evals.prompt: What does /northstar produce, and where does it save it?
evals.expected_output: A locked northstar.md at {vault}/context/<company>/northstar.md containing one leading North Star + 3-5 input levers + guardrails + revenue as a lagging output below the star + a mandatory validation plan.
expectations: Saves to context/<company>/northstar.md
Output is a tier hierarchy: North Star + 3-5 inputs + guardrails + revenue below
A validation plan is a required/first-class section of the output

evals.prompt: Is the framework-compliance audit a hard gate? What happens when the chosen star fails the 'customer value' criterion (e.g. an efficiency metric)?
evals.expected_output: The gate is SOFT, not a hard block. A star that fails a criterion is not rejected; the company must either fix it (go up a level) or justify it as a documented deviation mapped to a named framework exception (efficiency/transformation, qualified financial for mature post-PMF, or layered hierarchy). A documented, framework-mapped deviation passes the gate.
expectations: States the audit gate is soft, not a hard block
A failing criterion must be either fixed or justified as a documented deviation
Deviation must map to a named framework exception (efficiency/transformation, qualified financial, or layered hierarchy)
A documented framework-mapped deviation passes the gate

evals.prompt: Where does revenue sit in the North Star hierarchy, and why not use revenue as the star?
evals.expected_output: Revenue is a Tier-2 lagging output placed BELOW the star, never as the star itself. The North Star is leading; revenue is lagging ('the game is over before you've had a chance to play'). Revenue is tracked to confirm the star points the right way.
expectations: Revenue is placed below the star as a lagging output guardrail, never as the star
The North Star is a leading indicator; revenue is lagging
Revenue is used to validate the star points the right way

evals.prompt: How does /northstar choose the candidate metric shape, and when does it fall back to /grill?
evals.expected_output: It invokes /grill-fast (does not re-implement tree rendering) seeded with a generic archetype menu (customer-value default + engagement-usage + output-leverage + efficiency-transformation + axis-challenger). It falls back to /grill when the company has one obvious star and rendering 4 candidate shapes would be artificial.
expectations: Invokes /grill-fast rather than inlining/replicating tree rendering

Seeds grill-fast with a generic archetype menu (customer-value, engagement-usage, output-leverage, efficiency-transformation) plus the axis-challenger; the company picks per strategy and no archetype is forced as the answer
Falls back to /grill for a single-obvious-star / one-dimensional choice

evals.prompt: A company has 5 distinct lines of business. How should /northstar handle the North Star?
evals.expected_output: Keep ONE enterprise North Star measured per stream/line, rolled up VALUE-weighted (e.g. by revenue), never volume-weighted. Do not create co-equal stars. This layered hierarchy is the framework's documented exception — challenge the need first.
expectations: Keeps one enterprise star measured per stream, not co-equal stars
Roll-up is value-weighted (e.g. revenue), never volume-weighted
Layered hierarchy is the documented exception; challenge the need first

evals.prompt: What must the validation plan in the output contain?
evals.expected_output: At least one input->output causal experiment with a named method (A/B > staggered rollout/synthetic control > before/after cohort), plus the cheapest 'one-slice' check runnable now to confirm the star is measurable. The star is a hypothesis until the data backs it.
expectations: Requires >=1 input->output causal experiment
Names a causal method preference: A/B > staggered rollout > before/after cohort
Requires a cheapest one-slice check runnable now
Frames the locked star as a hypothesis until validated

evals.prompt: Should /northstar be used to draft this quarter's OKRs or build a metrics dashboard?
evals.expected_output: No. The North Star sits ABOVE OKRs (OKRs move its inputs); the skill is not for drafting OKRs, building dashboards, or per-feature/per-task metrics. It defines the single company-level aligning metric.
expectations: Does NOT handle OKR drafting; the North Star sits above OKRs
Does NOT handle dashboards or per-feature/per-task metrics
Scope is the single company-level North Star metric

evals.prompt: How does /northstar use the /audit skill, and is it mandatory?
evals.expected_output: The inline framework-compliance audit (7-point test + 7 rules + failure modes from references/north-star-framework.md) is the real gate. /audit is optional: it may recommend /audit p1 p4 as a cheap principle cross-check, off by default; it does not auto-run /audit --all every invocation (the generic principle audit does not check the NSM framework).
expectations: The inline framework audit (from references/) is the real gate, not /audit
/audit p1 p4 is optional and off by default
Does not mandatorily run /audit --all every invocation
